# noaa-recon-api

> Open-source HTTP API serving archival GOES satellite imagery and NOAA Tail
> Doppler Radar (TDR) data as georeferenced map tiles, plus a raw-netCDF
> passthrough for client-side rendering. CORS-open, no auth, free to call
> from any web app.

Live base URL: https://joshmurdock.net/api
Local dev base URL: http://127.0.0.1:8000
OpenAPI schema (machine-readable, authoritative): {base}/openapi.json
Interactive docs: {base}/docs

## Live endpoints

- GET /v1/health: Liveness check. Returns {"status":"ok"}.
- GET /v1/satellite/tile?time=ISO8601Z&band=13|9&satellite=goes-east|goes-west&cmap=default|abi13|abi9|bd|ir4|enhanced|nrl|grayscale[&center=lat,lon&dims=N&unit=nm|km&resolution_km=N]: Requests a georeferenced GOES IR/WV PNG tile for the nearest actual satellite scan to an arbitrary UTC timestamp (~10-min resolution). satellite=goes-east (default; GOES-16 until 2025-01-14, then GOES-19) or goes-west (GOES-17 until 2023-01-10, then GOES-18) auto-resolve to the right satellite for the date — covers ~2017-2018 onward only (pre-ABI storms like Katrina/2005 aren't reachable; different instrument, no open archive). Optionally pass center+dims to render a fast, high-detail box around a point instead of the slow/coarse full disk (native ~2km/px by default; resolution_km coarsens it). cmap=default (the default) resolves server-side to the correct per-band standard enhancement: abi13 for band=13, abi9 for band=9 — these are NOT interchangeable (different physical quantities, different color conventions); always let cmap default rather than hardcoding one band's table for the other band. Async: first call returns {"status":"generating","key":...}; poll the status endpoint below until ready. Full param/response reference: API.md#get-v1satellitetile
- GET /v1/satellite/status/{key}: Poll for a tile job's result. Returns status ready|generating|error|idle, and on ready: png_url (relative — prefix with the base URL), bounds (Leaflet-format [[lat_s,lon_w],[lat_n,lon_e]]), band, cmap, satellite, scan_start, and (for a bbox request) center, width_km, resolution_km.
- GET /v1/satellite/colortable?cmap=default&band=13|9: Returns the exact color stops ({temp_c, hex}) for a colortable, read from the same data the renderer uses (not a hardcoded copy) — use this to render a legend that's guaranteed to match the actual tile output.
- GET /demo/netcdf-three/: Static demo page rendering netCDF data client-side via Three.js/WebGL (netcdf-three library). Currently loads sample data only.

## Planned endpoints (return 501 today, shape documented for forward compatibility)

- GET /v1/tdr/missions, GET /v1/tdr/sweep: Tail Doppler Radar data from NOAA's raw aircraft archive. Not yet implemented — see API.md and app/services/tdr.py for the planned design.
- GET /v1/raw/netcdf: Server-side netCDF subsetting by center/dimensions for client-side rendering. Not yet implemented — see API.md and app/routers/raw.py.
- GET /v1/satellite/tile with band=2 (visible) or a geocolor product: Not yet accepted — only band=13 (Clean IR) and band=9 (Water Vapor) work today.

## Full documentation

- [API.md](API.md): Complete endpoint reference, response shapes, color table descriptions, and copy-pasteable integration examples in curl, JavaScript (Leaflet), and Python.
- [README.md](README.md): Setup, architecture, deployment, and contributor/agent notes (roadmap, conventions to keep, a documented reprojection bug already found and fixed).

## Integration quick-start (JavaScript)

```javascript
const data = await fetch('https://joshmurdock.net/api/v1/satellite/tile?time=' + new Date().toISOString() + '&band=13').then(r => r.json());
// poll https://joshmurdock.net/api/v1/satellite/status/{data.key} every ~3s until status is "ready",
// then: L.imageOverlay('https://joshmurdock.net/api' + data.png_url, data.bounds).addTo(map);
```

## Notes

- No auth, no API key, no enforced rate limit. Avoid polling status faster than every 3s.
- Identical requests (same band/cmap/satellite/resolved-scan-time, plus center/width_km/resolution_km for a bbox request) hit a server-side cache — cheap to repeat.
- Prefer center+dims over full-disk when you know roughly where you need imagery: measured ~11x faster processing (1.3s vs 14s for a 500km box) and ~130x smaller PNG. The initial ~25MB NOAA S3 download per scan is unaffected by bbox vs full-disk (not yet optimized).
- resolution_km can't go finer than sensor native (2km for bands 9/13) — finer requests are silently clamped up, not rejected.
- License: MIT.
